title: Eagle River Nature Center — Month of the Military Child Family Event
location: Eagle River Nature Center, Eagle River
description: Girl Scout Troop 21010 on JBER partners with ERNC for a family afternoon celebrating Month of the Military Child. Activities run 12–3 PM with a bear and moose safety talk at 1 PM.

title: Everyday Anchorage: Duke Russell & Ward Wells — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum at Rasmuson Center, Anchorage
description: Photography and painting exhibition capturing daily life in Anchorage through the work of two local artists, Ward Wells (photography) and Duke Russell (paintings). Opens May 1 and runs through early 2027.

title: Michaela Goade: Song of the Seasons — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum at Rasmuson Center, Anchorage
description: New exhibition featuring art, sketches, and process videos by Tlingit and Norwegian illustrator Michaela Goade, celebrating Alaska's seasons through color and story. Opens May 1.

title: AK Grown: Childhood in Alaska — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum at Rasmuson Center, Anchorage
description: Photographs from the 1930s to the present trace nearly a century of childhood across Alaska, exploring how young Alaskans experience the state's distinctive environment and culture.

title: 39th Annual Kenai Peninsula Sport, Rec & Trade Show
location: Soldotna Regional Sports Complex (Soldotna Field House), Soldotna
description: Annual three-day trade show featuring over 100 vendors showcasing RVs, boats, ATVs, fishing trips, and outdoor gear, plus archery, a youth fish pond, and a family fun zone. Opening night Friday 6–9 PM includes food and beverage with admission.
sourceName: Kenai Peninsula Sport, Rec & Trade Show

title: Yonder Mountain String Band
location: Atwood Concert Hall, Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, Anchorage
description: Progressive jamgrass band Yonder Mountain String Band performs in support of their album Nowhere Next, a blend of bluegrass, indie rock, and country. Presented by the Anchorage Concert Association.

title: Pink Cheeks Triathlon
location: Seward, AK
description: Sprint triathlon held in Seward in early May, one of the first of the season on the Kenai Peninsula.

title: 35th Annual Anchorage Salmon Run
location: Inlet View Elementary School, Anchorage
description: Annual 5K, 10K, and 2K fun run along the Tony Knowles Coastal Trail, starting and finishing at Inlet View Elementary. Raises funds for elementary school programs; followed by a Salmon Run Carnival.

title: Steps for Strong Minds 5K Run/Walk
location: Anchorage, AK
description: Family-focused mental health awareness 5K run/walk held in Anchorage, raising awareness and funds for mental health initiatives.

title: Turnagain Arm Trail Run
location: Turnagain Arm Trail, Anchorage
description: Evening trail run along the scenic Turnagain Arm Trail, one of the area's most popular running routes with stunning Inlet views.

title: Dance Production at Alaska Center for the Performing Arts
location: Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, Anchorage
description: A vibrant dance production celebrating the beauty of creativity found in everyday life, presented at the Alaska PAC.
sourceName: Alaska Center for the Performing Arts

title: Love a Nurse Run 5K
location: Anchorage, AK
description: A 5K run and walk benefiting the Foundation for Alaska Nurses, held annually to honor nurses during Nurses Week.

title: Girls on the Run 5K
location: Anchorage, AK
description: Girls on the Run celebratory 5K race focused on building confidence and accomplishment in young girls through running.

title: Jo Koy: Just Being Koy Tour
location: Alaska Airlines Center, Anchorage
description: Stand-up comedian Jo Koy performs his Just Being Koy Tour at the Alaska Airlines Center. Doors open at 7 PM; show starts at 8 PM. Ages 21+ unless accompanied by a parent or guardian.

title: America 250 — Alaska's Week of Dreams: 'Make It Monday' Forum
location: Anchorage Chamber of Commerce, Anchorage
description: Anchorage Chamber of Commerce forum celebrating the America250 national anniversary with a focus on Alaska's civic milestones and future vision.

title: Get Moving Mat-Su Valley Dash 5K
location: Wasilla, AK
description: Community 5K run/walk event in the Matanuska-Susitna Valley promoting active living.

title: Anchorage Bike to Work Day
location: Citywide, Anchorage
description: Annual Bike to Work Day encourages Anchorage residents to commute by bicycle. Part of national Bike Month, with pit stops and community celebration around the city.

title: Arctic Marine Science: Sikuliaq to Shore — Exhibition Opening
location: Anchorage Museum at Rasmuson Center, Anchorage
description: New exhibition inviting visitors aboard a recreated section of the research vessel Sikuliaq to explore Arctic marine science and the researchers who study Alaska's ocean ecosystems.

title: Chiodos Block Party at Williwaw Social Outdoors
location: Williwaw Social Outdoors, Anchorage
description: Outdoor block party on F Street headlined by post-hardcore band Chiodos, part of the summer outdoor concert series at Williwaw Social Outdoors.

title: Seward 5K & 10K Road Race
location: Seward, AK
description: Annual spring 5K and 10K road race held in Seward with 100–500 participants, one of the first road races of the season on the Kenai Peninsula.

title: Matson Ocean Education Center Grand Opening
location: Alaska Wildlife Conservation Center, Portage (Mile 79 Seward Hwy)
description: Grand opening of the Matson Ocean Education Center at AWCC, a new 1,000 sq ft interactive facility focused on Cook Inlet beluga whales, tides, and coastal habitats along Turnagain Arm.

title: Gold Nugget Triathlon
location: Bartlett High School, 1101 Golden Bear Drive, Anchorage
description: Women-only sprint triathlon with 1,650 in-person participants, dedicated to improving the lives of women and girls through athletics. Includes swim, bike, and run legs starting at Bartlett High School.

title: Shoreline Mafia at Williwaw
location: Williwaw, 609 F Street, Anchorage
description: West Coast rap group Shoreline Mafia performs at Williwaw in downtown Anchorage.

title: Alaska Arbor Day — Community Tree Planting
location: Various parks, Anchorage
description: Alaska Arbor Day volunteer event inviting community members to plant trees in Anchorage parks, organized by the Anchorage Park Foundation.

title: Gunsight Mountain Music Fest 2026
location: Gunsight Mountain Lodge, Chickaloon, AK
description: Three-day outdoor music festival headlined by Confederate Railroad at Gunsight Mountain Lodge near Chickaloon. Tickets range from $50–$245.

title: Trent Waldron Half Marathon & 10K
location: Margaret Eagan Sullivan Park, Westchester Lagoon, Anchorage
description: Anchorage Running Club's annual half marathon and 10K along Chester Creek and University Trails, honoring Marcie Trent and Larry Waldron. Start/finish at Westchester Lagoon; half starts at 9 AM, 10K at 9:20 AM.

title: 3 Rings Circus at Sullivan Arena
location: Sullivan Arena, 1600 Gambell Street, Anchorage
description: Two-day family circus event at the Sullivan Arena featuring acrobatics, clowns, and big-top performances. Multiple shows across Saturday and Sunday.

title: Sawyer Brown & Montgomery Gentry
location: Alaska Airlines Center, 3550 Providence Drive, Anchorage
description: Country music double-bill featuring Sawyer Brown and Montgomery Gentry at the Alaska Airlines Center.

title: Anchorage Museum — Memorial Day Free Admission
location: Anchorage Museum at Rasmuson Center, Anchorage
description: Free admission for current and past military members and up to five family members on Memorial Day, May 25, 2026.

title: Purity Ring
location: Wendy Williamson Auditorium, 2533 Providence Drive, Anchorage
description: Canadian electronic music duo Purity Ring performs at the Wendy Williamson Auditorium on the UAA campus.

title: Little Explorers Toddler Hike at ERNC
location: Eagle River Nature Center, Eagle River
description: Weekly toddler-paced guided hike on or near the Rodak Trail at the Eagle River Nature Center, targeting children up to age 6 with accompanying adults. No registration required; check in by 11 AM.

title: Chase Matthew at Sullivan Arena Parking Lot
location: Sullivan Arena Parking Lot, 1600 Gambell Street, Anchorage
description: Country artist Chase Matthew performs an outdoor concert at the Sullivan Arena parking lot in downtown Anchorage.

title: Twilight 12K & Skinny Mini 6K
location: 8th & H Street (start) / Rose Garden, Delaney Park Strip (finish), Anchorage
description: Popular evening run featuring a scenic 12K or 6K course through downtown Anchorage, closing the A/C Street bridge and traversing the Coastal Trail, with live bands along the route and a post-race party at the Rose Garden.

title: Anchorage Tacos and Tequila Fest
location: Sullivan Arena Parking Lot, 1600 Gambell Street, Anchorage
description: Outdoor food and drink festival celebrating tacos and tequila with vendors, music, and entertainment in the Sullivan Arena parking lot.

title: Golden Girls: The Laughs Continue
location: Atwood Concert Hall, Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, Anchorage
description: Comedy and nostalgia touring production celebrating the beloved Golden Girls television series, performed at Atwood Concert Hall.
